includes: Deck identity and project intent summary.
Card-level composition facts for deck version v1.0.
Functional role and role-category distribution over the playable 100 cards.
Neutral structural strengths and pressure points.
excludes: Card cut, add, swap, or replacement recommendations.
Power scores, rankings, tier placements, or final verdicts.

method_notes: Counts cover the playable 100 cards: the commander plus 99 main-deck entries (Island appears twice; all other names are unique).
The 7 SIDEBOARD cards from the source import are summarized separately and are not included in the playable-100 counts.
A card counts once per role it carries and once per category in which it carries at least one role; multi-role cards therefore appear in multiple counters.
Flavor-name imports (for example Bridge of Khazad-dum) are counted under their canonical card facts via recorded alias resolution.
All role data comes from first-pass human-curated assignments; 87 of the 100 playable cards carry high-confidence assignments and 13 carry medium-confidence assignments.

deck_identity_summary.stated_identity: An artifact combo-control engine disguised as a Myr tribal deck.
deck_identity_summary.stated_resource_model: Myr and artifact bodies are used as resources, not merely as attackers.

category_distribution.counting_basis: cards_with_any_role and cards_with_primary_role count distinct cards in the playable 100; role_assignments_in_category counts role assignments, so multi-role cards contribute more than once

section_summaries.commander_and_project_intent: Urtet, Remnant of Memnarch is a five-color-identity Myr commander whose abilities reward casting Myr spells, untapping Myr, and distributing +1/+1 counters. The project brief frames the deck as an artifact combo-control engine that preserves a Myr typal identity, with Sprint 1 goals of improving explosiveness, consistency, mana fixing, and resilience.
section_summaries.mana_development: 28 of 100 playable cards carry at least one Mana Development role, covering 47 in-category role assignments: 14 mana rocks, 14 ramp cards, 9 cost-reduction effects, 5 mana-fixing effects, and 5 utility-land mana contributions. The layer is concentrated in artifact-based acceleration. No card currently carries the land_ramp or ritual_or_burst_mana roles, so acceleration is almost entirely permanent-based rather than land-based or single-turn burst.
section_summaries.card_advantage_and_selection: 11 cards carry a Card Advantage role (4 repeatable draw, 2 burst draw, 4 impulse or selection, 1 graveyard card access) and 16 cards carry a Selection and Tutoring role, covering 20 in-category role assignments (7 artifact tutors, 5 broad tutors, 4 card filtering, 3 topdeck manipulation, 1 land tutor). Selection density is weighted toward finding specific artifacts rather than accumulating raw card quantity.
section_summaries.interaction: 15 cards carry an Interaction role, covering 16 in-category role assignments: 4 counterspells, 4 board wipes, 2 targeted removal effects, 2 bounce effects, 2 stax or hate pieces, and 2 graveyard hate effects. The interaction suite leans toward stack-based answers and mass removal, with a comparatively small count of single-target permanent answers.
section_summaries.protection_and_resilience: 8 cards carry a Protection and Resilience role in the playable 100, covering 11 in-category role assignments: 4 broad protection, 3 indestructible or regeneration, 2 hexproof or shroud, and 2 defensive pillowfort effects. No playable card currently carries the anti_wipe_protection role; the only card assigned that role (Darksteel Forge) sits in the imported SIDEBOARD section.
section_summaries.recursion_and_recovery: 5 cards carry a Recursion and Recovery role, and all recursion is artifact-directed (artifact_recursion). No card carries creature_recursion, graveyard_recursion, or permanent_recursion, so recovery capacity is concentrated on returning artifacts.
section_summaries.engine_and_scaling: 14 cards carry an Engine and Scaling role, covering 24 in-category role assignments: 6 tap-untap engines, 6 scaling threats, 5 payoff engines, 3 artifact engines, 3 mana engines, and 1 token engine. The deck appears structurally dense in repeatable artifact-driven value loops, consistent with the stated engine identity.
section_summaries.combo_and_win_conditions: 5 cards carry a Combo and Win Conditions role, covering 6 in-category role assignments: 5 damage payoffs and 1 combo payoff (Aetherflux Reservoir, which carries both). No card currently carries the combo_piece, combo_enabler, finisher, or alternate_win_condition roles. Given the stated combo-control archetype, the gap between the archetype description and the tagged win-condition structure is a primary area to investigate: it may reflect first-pass Knowledge coverage rather than the deck's actual structure.
section_summaries.board_presence: 24 cards carry a Board Presence role, almost entirely as creature_body (23) plus 3 token producers, two of which are also creature bodies. No card carries evasive_threat, blocker, or commander_support. Board presence is therefore functional mass rather than combat-oriented pressure, consistent with bodies-as-resources.
section_summaries.lands_and_mana_base: 34 lands support a five-color commander identity, carrying 66 land-role assignments: 21 colored sources, 19 colorless sources, 12 fixing lands, 7 playable utility lands (an 8th, Mistrise Village, is in the sideboard), 6 artifact lands, 1 fetch-or-search land, and the 3-land Urza's Mine/Power Plant/Tower set. Only 7 lands are basics (including two Islands and one snow-covered Island) while 23 nonland cards have colored costs, so colored-cast consistency is a defined area for later investigation.
section_summaries.sideboard: The imported SIDEBOARD holds 7 cards whose roles skew toward defense and disruption (protection, pillowfort, stax, bounce, board wipe). Commander play typically has no sideboard, so the intended relationship between these cards and the 100-card deck is an open structural question.

structural_strengths: High artifact density (50 of 100 playable cards) that structurally supports the deck's artifact engines, cost reducers, improvise costs, and artifact-count payoffs.
A deep mana development layer (28 cards carrying 47 mana-development role assignments) built on redundant mana rocks and cost reduction, matching the explosiveness goal in the brief.
Dedicated artifact tutoring (7 cards) plus broad tutors (5) that give the deck structural access to specific engine pieces.
Redundant untap effects (6 tap-untap engines) that can reuse mana rocks and activated abilities, consistent with the engine identity.
An artifact recursion package (5 cards) that lets the deck reuse its primary resource type after disruption.
A low, engine-oriented curve (average nonland mana value 3.0, with 49 of 66 nonland cards at mana value 3 or less).
structural_pressure_points: Win-condition structure is under-described in current Knowledge: no card carries finisher, combo_piece, combo_enabler, or alternate_win_condition, while the brief describes a combo-control archetype. Whether this is a Knowledge coverage gap or a structural gap needs investigation.
The card advantage layer is comparatively small (11 cards, 6 with a primary card-advantage role), which is relevant to the brief's consistency goal.
Colored-cast consistency for a five-color identity is unverified: 23 nonland cards have colored costs against 21 colored land sources and 12 fixing lands, with heavy colorless production alongside.
Anti-wipe resilience is absent from the playable 100 (the only anti_wipe_protection card is in the sideboard), which is relevant to the brief's resilience goal for a board-dependent engine deck.
Recursion is exclusively artifact-directed; the deck has no tagged means of recovering non-artifact cards.
Targeted removal is a small share of interaction (2 of 15 interaction cards), concentrating answers in counterspells and board wipes.
Myr typal density is 14 of 100 cards; how reliably this supports the commander's Myr-cast and Myr-untap triggers is untested.
The 7-card SIDEBOARD has no defined role in Commander play and its status relative to the 100-card deck is unresolved.
open_questions: Which specific card combinations constitute the deck's intended combo lines, and which cards would carry combo_piece or combo_enabler once synergy mapping exists as a later Knowledge task?
Does Aetherflux Reservoir (the sole combo_payoff) have sufficient structural support to function as a win route, and which cards feed it?
How often can the deck cast its 23 colored nonland cards on curve, given the land mix? This is a question for later simulation.
How frequently does the commander's Myr-focused trigger set find targets, given 14 Myr typal cards?
Is the absence of land-based ramp and burst mana a deliberate structural identity or an artifact of the current list?
What is the intended function of the SIDEBOARD section in this project's workflow?
Do the 13 medium-confidence role assignments hold up under a focused Knowledge review?

Walking Ballista sits in the main deck per the source import; its structural role in the engine (scaling threat and damage payoff) versus its wider reputation is deliberately not evaluated here and awaits synergy mapping.

explicit_non_recommendation_boundary.statement: This document is a baseline structural analysis only. It makes no card cut, add, swap, replacement, upgrade, budget, or purchase suggestions; it assigns no power score, rank, tier, or verdict; it reports no simulation or matchup results; and it changes no decklist, version, knowledge, or card-data files. Recommendations are a separate later task and must not cite this document as having pre-authorized any change.
explicit_non_recommendation_boundary.next_layer: Future recommendation analysis is a separate task that will consume this baseline.
